content.full_text:
F,rrn PolrrrcAr- PnACTTcES C ovMrssroN
-128 J Street e Suite 610 o Sacramento. CA 95811-2-129
(916) 3ll--s660 r Fax (916) -122-0886
August 12, 2008
William Cavala
1429 Perkins Way
Sacramento. CA 95818
Re
Your Request for Advice
Our File No. W-08-124
Dear Mr. Cavala:
This letter responds to yollr request for advice on behalf of Mariko Yamada
regarding the campaign provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").' Yout
questions involve the reporting of a payment made by Ms. Yamada to settle a legal
dispute with a former campaign worker.
The Act requires the Commission to provide written advice to any person whose
duties under the Act are in question or to that person's authorized representative.
(Section 83 I 14(b); Regulation 18329(a) (copy enclosed).) However, the Commission
may decline to provide written advice where the requestor is seeking advice that tequires
us to evalLrate conduct that has already occurred. (Regulation 18329(b)(8XA).)
Additionally, the Commission may decline to provide written advice if providing advice
would be inappropriate or otherwise not in the public interest. (Regulation
1832e(b)(8XF).)
After a careful review of the facts you provided, we decline to provide written
advice to you at this time. The questions you have posed are related to a past
enforcement action, FPPC Case No. 08/351, resolved by the Commission's Enforcement
Division with an Advisory Letter to Ms. Yamada dated July 1, 2008. Accordingly,
issuing advice at this time would relate to past conduct and would rlot further the
impartial, effective administration of the Act so mandated by section 831 I l.
As instructed in the Advisory Letter to Ms. Yamada, the committee's campaign
statement should be amended immediately to reflect the paymerlt to settle the dispute as a
I The Prtlitical Retirrm Act is contained irr Cirvernment Code Sections 8l(XX) throu-uh 91014. ,\ll
statutory ret'erences are ttl the Cio'"'ernment Code. unless otherrvise indicatecl. The regulations tlf the Fair
P<t litical Practices Comnr iss ion are contained in Sections I l{ I I 0 throLrgh I 1t997 of Title I of the Calitirrn ia
(hdeofRegulations.;\ll regulatoryre't'elencesaretoTitle2.Division6oftheCalitirrnilClodeof
Regulations, unless tltherwise indicated.


File No. W-08-124
Page No. 2
campaign expenditure. Furthermore, because Ms. Yamada has previously made a
payment of personal funds to the settle the dispute, the committee's campaign statement
should be amended immediately to reflect a contribution from Ms. Yamada to the
committee equal to the amount of the payment made to settle the dispute.
You may contact our Technical Assistance Division if you need additional
assistance with the necessary amendments. If you believe you can provide additional
facts relating to the past enforcement action, FPPC Case No. 08/351, you may provide
these facts to our Enforcement Division for further consideration.
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.
Sincerely,
Scott Hallabrin
General Counsel
By: Brian G. Lau
Counsel, Legal Division
BGLjgl
